headline: How Erdogan's regime built a crony capitalist system in Turkey
description: In less than a quarter of a century, the Islamist-conservative party of Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan handed over the keys of the country's economy to a small circle drawn from a loyal business class and clientelist networks.

Iran and US say deal 'has never been closer' despite Trump lashing out
description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said a draft deal to end the US-Israeli war on Iran was nearing completion, after Donald Trump earlier dismissed an Iranian account of the draft agreement as lies, bearing 'no relation to the truth.'

headline: Elon Musk's role was 'instrumental' in the Belfast riots, researchers say
description: The newly crowned world's first trillionaire is once again facing criticism over the violent narratives he amplified on his platform X.

headline: Jeanne Damas celebrates Rouje's 10th anniversary, her brand embodying a certain ideal of 'the Parisienne'
description: The 34-year-old has showcased her youth and creative world since the earliest days of social media. To mark the 10th birthday of her ready-to-wear clothing and beauty brand, she is launching a special collection and opening a coffee shop.

headline: Josh O'Connor, star of Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day,' wants to 'cultivate his garden'
description: At 36, he is one of the most sought-after actors in both independent cinema and major Hollywood productions. In just a few years, the British artist, who is passionate about gardening, has put his delicate masculinity and subtle acting style to work for a wide range of characters, from a lovesick farmer to Prince Charles or a priest.

The murky ties between FIFA and the US
description: After the 2022 World Cup was awarded to Qatar, the US launched a legal offensive against several FIFA members before securing the co-hosting of the 2026 tournament. This is the story of the rise of American soft power in global football.

headline: 'Lyhanna was a victim twice': Women-led rallies across France condemn failures after schoolgirl's death
description: Several thousand people gathered on Monday in front of courthouses and city halls to express their dismay at the judicial treatment of sexual violence committed against children. Many demonstrators called for the resignation of Justice Minister Gérald Darmanin.

headline: Bardella's contradiction of Le Pen on pensions confounds French far right
description: Jordan Bardella, who would stand as the Rassemblement National's presidential candidate if Marine Le Pen's election ban is upheld, has broken with his boss's line on a crucial policy issue.
